docs.title: NOMAD Gallery | Highlights
docs.text:
Welcome to NOMAD Gallery — a space to showcase innovative features, uploads, and use cases created with the NOMAD platform. Whether you want to share your latest work or explore what’s possible, this is the place to engage, discover, and inspire.

🌟 Featured highlights

Hand-picked examples that demonstrate what’s possible with NOMAD — from large-scale databases to innovative workflows.

docs.title: The Alexandria Database hosted by the NOMAD Calculations App
docs.text:
The Alexandria database is being ingested into NOMAD! The result is tens of millions of NOMAD calculations entries that can be efficiently queried and explored via NOMAD's extensive search and filtering functionalities.

Submitter: Joseph Rudzinski

Submitted: 2025-02-17

docs.title: Search interface for the perovskite solar cell database
docs.text:
Field: Photovoltaics, Inst: Humboldt-Universität zu Berlin, (DE)

A search interface for exploring the perovskite solar cell database using materials, composition, and device-property filters to support comparison and analysis of photovoltaic performance.

Submitter: Pepe MarquezCoauthors: testMethodology: ExperimentalTechnique: perovskite solar cell fabrication, photovoltaic device characterizationData Size: 1.5 TB Impact: 3000 UsersFunding: Deutsche Forschungsgemeinschaft (DFG) - Project 460197019Publication: https://fairmat-nfdi.github.io/nomad-perovskite-solar-cells-databaseKeywords: Perovskites Solar cells Energy materials Materials database 📄Submitted: 2025-02-19

docs.title: NOMAD Gallery | Explore
docs.text:
Explore the NOMAD Gallery

Browse community-submitted use cases showing how NOMAD is used in real research workflows. Use the filters below to explore by methodology, research field, or keywords.

Methodology Research field Country Keywords Sort Clear Visualization of a lipid bilayer according to the coarse-grained MARTINI model •

The NOMAD visualizer now has support for non-atomic particles (i.e., particles representing something other than a chemical element).

By Joseph Rudzinski 2025-02-15 View Details Keywords Ionic Liquids under shear Max-Planck Institute for Polymer Research • DE Material Science Computational

docs.title: NOMAD Gallery | Home
docs.text:
Featured highlights

Hand-picked examples that demonstrate what's possible with NOMAD — from large-scale databases to innovative workflows.

The Alexandria Database hosted by the NOMAD Calculations App See more The Alexandria Database hosted by the NOMAD Calculations App

The Alexandria database is being ingested into NOMAD! The result is tens of millions of NOMAD calculations entries that can be efficiently queried and explored via NOMAD's extensive search and filtering functionalities.

Search interface for the perovskite solar cell database See more Search interface for the perovskite solar cell database

A search interface to explore the perovskite solar cell database based on materials and device properties.

Visualization of a lipid bilayer according to the coarse-grained MARTINI model See more Visualization of a lipid bilayer according to the coarse-grained MARTINI model

The NOMAD visualizer now has support for non-atomic particles (i.e., particles representing something other than a chemical element).

docs.text: The reference section includes all CLI commands and arguments, all configuration options, the possible schema annotations and their arguments, and a glossary of used terms.

docs.title: NOMAD Gallery | Submit
docs.text:
We welcome contributions to the NOMAD gallery!

To submit a new entry, simply fill out the submission form.

The form will guide you through the required information to ensure your submission follows the correct format.

Once submitted, a team member will review your entry to make sure everything looks good and will take care of publishing it. You will receive a confirmation once your submission has been processed.

If you have any questions, feel free to reach out through our Discord server or email us.
